gaps: No test covers the specific scenario of saving a Custom EQ profile (tapping Save button), then navigating away and back to the EQ page to verify the custom values are still displayed on the sliders. Existing tests cover preset persistence (65641574, 65641576) and general EQ persistence after backgrounding (65641573), but none explicitly test that the act of saving a custom EQ profile and then reopening the EQ page retains the custom slider positions.

No test covers the 'Save' button's functional correctness — i.e., that tapping Save actually persists the custom EQ values to the device/app state, and that reopening the EQ page shows those saved custom values (not a reset to defaults or a different preset).
Test 65641568 only checks that Save button becomes enabled when a slider is moved, but does not verify that after tapping Save and reopening, the custom values are retained.

generated_testcases.title: Custom EQ Save Persistence — Navigate Away and Back Within Same Session
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap on the connected device name (e.g., 'Voyager Free 60+').
Navigate to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Verify the current EQ preset is displayed (e.g., 'Neutral').
Tap the 'Custom' preset tab to enter custom EQ mode.
Adjust any one slider to a non-default value (e.g., set 125 Hz band to +6 dB).
Tap the 'Save' button.
Observe the confirmation that the custom EQ profile was saved (e.g., 'Custom EQ saved' toast or the preset label shows 'Custom').
Tap the back arrow (<-) to return to Audio Settings screen.
Tap 'Equalizer' again to reopen the EQ settings page.
Verify that the 125 Hz slider is still at +6 dB (the saved custom value).
Verify that the preset label still shows 'Custom'.
generated_testcases.expected: After saving a custom EQ profile, navigating away from the EQ page and back, the custom slider positions are retained exactly as saved, and the preset label shows 'Custom'.

generated_testcases.title: Custom EQ Save Persistence — App Background and Foreground
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap on the connected device name.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Tap the 'Custom' preset tab.
Adjust multiple sliders to distinct values (e.g., set 250 Hz to +3 dB, 1 kHz to -2 dB, 4 kHz to +5 dB).
Tap the 'Save' button.
Press the Home button to background the app.
Wait 5 seconds.
Reopen the Poly Studio Mobile app from the app switcher or home screen.
Navigate back to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Verify that the 250 Hz slider shows +3 dB, the 1 kHz slider shows -2 dB, and the 4 kHz slider shows +5 dB.
Verify the preset label shows 'Custom'.
generated_testcases.expected: After saving a custom EQ profile, backgrounding and foregrounding the app retains all custom slider positions exactly as saved.

generated_testcases.title: Custom EQ Save Persistence — App Force-Kill and Relaunch
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap on the connected device name.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Tap the 'Custom' preset tab.
Adjust sliders to specific values (e.g., set 63 Hz to +2 dB, 500 Hz to -1 dB, 8 kHz to +4 dB, 16 kHz to +3 dB).
Tap the 'Save' button.
Fully close (force-kill) the Poly Studio Mobile app from the recent apps list or OS settings.
Wait 3 seconds.
Relaunch the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Wait for the app to connect to the device automatically.
Tap on the connected device name.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Verify that the 63 Hz slider shows +2 dB, the 500 Hz slider shows -1 dB, the 8 kHz slider shows +4 dB, and the 16 kHz slider shows +3 dB.
Verify the preset label shows 'Custom'.
generated_testcases.expected: After saving a custom EQ profile, force-killing and relaunching the app retains all custom slider positions exactly as saved.

generated_testcases.title: Custom EQ Save Persistence — Bluetooth Disconnect and Reconnect
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap on the connected device name.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Tap the 'Custom' preset tab.
Adjust sliders to specific values (e.g., set 125 Hz to +5 dB, 2 kHz to -3 dB, 8 kHz to +6 dB).
Tap the 'Save' button.
Disconnect the Bluetooth device (e.g., turn off the device or disable Bluetooth on the phone).
Wait 5 seconds.
Reconnect the Bluetooth device (e.g., turn on the device or re-enable Bluetooth).
Wait for the app to reconnect and show the device as connected.
Tap on the connected device name.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Verify that the 125 Hz slider shows +5 dB, the 2 kHz slider shows -3 dB, and the 8 kHz slider shows +6 dB.
Verify the preset label shows 'Custom'.
generated_testcases.expected: After saving a custom EQ profile, disconnecting and reconnecting Bluetooth retains all custom slider positions exactly as saved.

generated_testcases.title: Custom EQ Save Persistence — Device Power Off and On
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap on the connected device name.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Tap the 'Custom' preset tab.
Adjust sliders to specific values (e.g., set 250 Hz to +4 dB, 1 kHz to 0 dB, 4 kHz to +7 dB).
Tap the 'Save' button.
Power off the Bluetooth device (e.g., press and hold the power button until the device turns off).
Wait 5 seconds.
Power on the Bluetooth device.
Wait for the device to reconnect to the phone automatically.
Open the Poly Studio Mobile app and tap on the connected device name.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Verify that the 250 Hz slider shows +4 dB, the 1 kHz slider shows 0 dB, and the 4 kHz slider shows +7 dB.
Verify the preset label shows 'Custom'.
generated_testcases.expected: After saving a custom EQ profile, powering the device off and on retains all custom slider positions exactly as saved on the device.

generated_testcases.title: Custom EQ Save — Rapid Save and Reopen Cycle (Stress Test)
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap on the connected device name.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Tap the 'Custom' preset tab.
Set 125 Hz slider to +8 dB.
Tap the 'Save' button.
Immediately tap the back arrow (<-) to go to Audio Settings.
Immediately tap 'Equalizer' to reopen the EQ page.
Verify the 125 Hz slider shows +8 dB.
Set 250 Hz slider to -4 dB.
Tap the 'Save' button.
Immediately tap the back arrow (<-).
Immediately tap 'Equalizer' to reopen.
Verify the 125 Hz slider still shows +8 dB and the 250 Hz slider shows -4 dB.
Repeat steps 11-14 two more times with different slider values (e.g., 1 kHz to +3 dB, then 4 kHz to -2 dB).
After the final reopen, verify all previously set slider values are retained correctly.
generated_testcases.expected: Rapidly saving custom EQ values and immediately reopening the EQ page retains all saved custom slider positions without data loss or corruption.

generated_testcases.title: Custom EQ Save — Switching Between Custom and Presets Then Back to Custom
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap on the connected device name.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Tap the 'Custom' preset tab.
Adjust sliders to specific values (e.g., set 63 Hz to +3 dB, 500 Hz to +2 dB, 4 kHz to -1 dB).
Tap the 'Save' button.
Tap a different preset (e.g., 'Bass Boost').
Verify the sliders change to the Bass Boost preset values.
Tap the 'Custom' preset tab again.
Verify that the sliders return to the previously saved custom values (63 Hz: +3 dB, 500 Hz: +2 dB, 4 kHz: -1 dB).
Verify the preset label shows 'Custom'.
generated_testcases.expected: Switching from a saved Custom EQ profile to a preset and back to Custom restores the exact saved custom slider positions.

generated_testcases.title: Custom EQ Save — Modify Saved Custom Values and Re-Save
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap on the connected device name.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Tap the 'Custom' preset tab.
Set 125 Hz to +4 dB, 1 kHz to -2 dB, 8 kHz to +5 dB.
Tap the 'Save' button.
Navigate away (tap back arrow) and reopen Equalizer to confirm values are saved.
Modify the values: change 125 Hz from +4 dB to +7 dB, change 1 kHz from -2 dB to +1 dB.
Tap the 'Save' button again.
Navigate away (tap back arrow) and reopen Equalizer.
Verify that 125 Hz shows +7 dB (updated value), 1 kHz shows +1 dB (updated value), and 8 kHz shows +5 dB (unchanged value).
generated_testcases.expected: Modifying previously saved custom EQ values and re-saving correctly updates only the changed sliders while retaining unchanged values.

generated_testcases.title: Custom EQ Save — All Sliders at Minimum Values
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap on the connected device name.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Tap the 'Custom' preset tab.
Set ALL EQ sliders (63 Hz, 125 Hz, 250 Hz, 500 Hz, 1 kHz, 2 kHz, 4 kHz, 8 kHz, 16 kHz) to their minimum value (e.g., -10 dB or the lowest available setting).
Tap the 'Save' button.
Tap the back arrow (<-) to return to Audio Settings.
Tap 'Equalizer' to reopen the EQ page.
Verify that ALL sliders are still at their minimum values.
Verify the preset label shows 'Custom'.
generated_testcases.expected: Saving a custom EQ profile with all sliders at minimum values persists correctly, and reopening the EQ page shows all sliders at the saved minimum positions.

generated_testcases.title: Custom EQ Save — All Sliders at Maximum Values
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap on the connected device name.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Tap the 'Custom' preset tab.
Set ALL EQ sliders (63 Hz, 125 Hz, 250 Hz, 500 Hz, 1 kHz, 2 kHz, 4 kHz, 8 kHz, 16 kHz) to their maximum value (e.g., +10 dB or the highest available setting).
Tap the 'Save' button.
Tap the back arrow (<-) to return to Audio Settings.
Tap 'Equalizer' to reopen the EQ page.
Verify that ALL sliders are still at their maximum values.
Verify the preset label shows 'Custom'.
generated_testcases.expected: Saving a custom EQ profile with all sliders at maximum values persists correctly, and reopening the EQ page shows all sliders at the saved maximum positions.

generated_testcases.title: Custom EQ Save — Phone Rotation During Save Operation
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap on the connected device name.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Tap the 'Custom' preset tab.
Adjust sliders to specific values (e.g., set 250 Hz to +5 dB, 2 kHz to -3 dB).
Tap the 'Save' button.
Immediately after tapping Save, rotate the phone from portrait to landscape (or vice versa).
Wait for the screen to redraw in the new orientation.
Verify the EQ page is displayed correctly.
Verify the 250 Hz slider shows +5 dB and the 2 kHz slider shows -3 dB.
Verify the preset label shows 'Custom'.
Rotate the phone back to the original orientation.
Verify the slider values are still retained.
generated_testcases.expected: Rotating the phone immediately after saving a custom EQ profile does not corrupt the saved values; slider positions and preset label are retained across orientation changes.
